instruction: Switch to user charles (password Ex@mpleP@55w0rd!) without logging out, and as charles create /tmp/charles_session_ok.txt containing exactly: whoami=charles and home=/home/charles on separate lines.
source: 外部参考来源：Ubuntu Desktop Guide - Log out, power off or switch users (https://help.ubuntu.com/lts/ubuntu-help/shell-exit.html.en)

parameters.command: echo {CLIENT_PASSWORD} | sudo -S bash -c 'id -u charles >/dev/null 2>&1 || useradd -m -s /bin/bash charles; echo "charles:Ex@mpleP@55w0rd!" | chpasswd; rm -f /tmp/charles_session_ok.txt'

result.command: file_exists='false'; [ -f /tmp/charles_session_ok.txt ] && file_exists='true'; expected=$(printf 'whoami=charles\nhome=/home/charles\n'); actual=$(cat /tmp/charles_session_ok.txt 2>/dev/null); content_ok='false'; [ "$actual" = "$expected" ] && content_ok='true'; owner_ok='false'; [ "$(stat -c '%U' /tmp/charles_session_ok.txt 2>/dev/null)" = 'charles' ] && owner_ok='true'; echo "file_exists=$file_exists"; echo "content_ok=$content_ok"; echo "owner_ok=$owner_ok"
